sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

itinerary.note: Junction toward GlennieRiver Road east Oscoda

paragraphs: The section of the byway that follows M-65 was first added to the state highway system in the mid-1930s. On December 20, 1988, the National Forest Service designated the River Road National Forest Scenic Byway in Iosco County. A second designation was added on September 22, 2005, when the Federal Highway Administration listed the roadway as the River Road National Scenic Byway.

gallery.desc: Bright, bold signs welcome visitors to River Road National Scenic Byway and National Forest Scenic Byway and the Huron-Manistee National Forests . The unique signing along the River Road section of Michigan highway M-65 lets travelers know they are entering a special area within the forest.
Trees line a wintry stretch of M-65. Byway travelers will experience a working national forest including tree harvesting, prescribed burning, wildfire control, and wildlife habitat improvement.

gallery.cap: River Road Scenic Byway An Entrance to Huron National Forest
gallery.desc: The National Forest sign welcomes visitors into the fall dappled forest along Highway 72. Location: Huron National Forest, Michigan Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: River Road Scenic Byway Busy Day at the Wright Lake Trailhead
gallery.desc: Skiers gather at the Wright Lake Trailhead for a day of cross country skiing on the Corsair Trail System. Location: Michigan Status: Public domain. Photo provided by Gordon Haase

gallery.cap: River Road Scenic Byway Tailwater Fishing Area below Cooke Dam
gallery.desc: The waters of the Cooke Dam lap up against the artificial shores. Location: Cooke Dam, on the AuSable River, Michigan Status: Public domain. Photo courtesy of Consumers Energy

gallery.cap: River Road Scenic Byway The Au Sable River Queen
gallery.desc: The River Queen, the only paddlewheel river boat operating in northern Michigan, take off from the docks. Location: Au Sable River, Michigan Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: River Road Scenic Byway Westgate Welcome Center Orientation Kiosk
gallery.desc: The many panels of an orientation kiosk offer helpful information at the Westgate Welcome Center. The Westgate and Eastgate Welcome Centers have large maps showing the Byway route, features, trails, and visitor facilities. Interpretive panels tell about historical events and natural features of the Au Sable River Valley. Location: Westgate Welcome Center, Michigan Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: River Road Scenic Byway Westgate Welcome Center Viewing Deck
gallery.desc: At Westgate Welcome Center, travelers will view Loud Pond of the Au Sable River from two observation decks. Location: Westgate Welcome Center, Au Sable River, Michigan Status: Public domain.
